steps.title: Find the singular subject
steps.content: The subject of the sentence is "Anthropologist Livia Barbosa," a single person. The phrase "of the Universidade Federal Fluminense in Brazil" only describes Barbosa, so it does not change the subject's number.

steps.title: Match subject and verb
steps.content: Choice A is a base-form verb used with plural subjects. Choice B uses "are," which is plural. Choice D uses "have," also plural. A singular third-person present-tense subject needs the singular verb form ending in -s. The answer is C.
